raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased merchandise through Best Buys website using my Best Buy Visa Platinum credit card issued by Citibank. Best Buy selected the delivery carriers ( XXXX XXXX XXXX I did not choose them. On XX/XX/year>, the carriers claimed the package was delivered, but I never received it at my address. No signature was obtained. The only delivery evidence is a blurry photo that does not show my residence or confirm valid delivery. 

When I contacted Best Buy customer service, I was told I would receive a refund. I waited in good faith, but later Best Buy reversed their position and refused the refund. I filed a police report and disputed the charge with Best Buy, Citibank, and the credit bureaus. 

I escalated the dispute to the executive teams of Best Buy and Citibank, providing detailed evidence. Best Buy refused to reconsider, relying on a " destination contract '' that I was never provided at purchase or in any debt validation materials. Best Buy incorrectly claimed I chose the carrier, which is untrue. Best Buys internal policy does not override federal law or excuse failure to provide valid proof of delivery. 

Citibank accepted Best Buys position without conducting a reasonable investigation as required by the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA, 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ) ) and reported inaccurate information to the credit bureaus. They failed to mark the account as disputed as required ( FCRA, 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 3 ) ). XXXX XXXX XXXX attempted collection without providing new or meaningful debt validation and continued to call me despite being informed that I dispute the debt ( potential violation of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, 15 U.S.C. 1692g ( b ) ). 

I disputed this inaccurate account with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  sent me mailed dispute results ( included ). XXXX  did not provide mailed confirmation. 

The attached documents provide the timeline, communications, and evidence supporting my complaint.
